sourcePage.rawText:
9   A student wishes to evaluate the function f(x) = x sin x, where x is in radians, but
    has a calculator that only works in degrees.

    What could the student type into their calculator to correctly evaluate f(4) ?

    A (π × 4 ÷ 180) × sin(4)

    B (π × 4 ÷ 180) × sin(π × 4 ÷ 180)

    C 4 × sin(π × 4 ÷ 180)

    D (180 × 4 ÷ π) × sin(4)

    E (180 × 4 ÷ π) × sin(180 × 4 ÷ π)

    F 4 × sin(180 × 4 ÷ π)




                                            11

stem.rawText:
A student wishes to evaluate the function f(x) = x sin x, where x is in radians, but
    has a calculator that only works in degrees.

    What could the student type into their calculator to correctly evaluate f(4) ?

solution.rawText:
The student wishes to evaluate f(4). Writing 4 rad to explicitly indicate radians, this becomes

                               f(4) = 4 sin(4 rad) = 4 × sin(4 rad).

Now
                                          π rad = 180◦ ,
so dividing by π gives
                                                    180◦
                                          1 rad =        .
                                                     π
Multiplying this by 4 then gives
                                                  4 × 180◦
                                        4 rad =            .
                                                     π
Therefore the student could type into their calculator

                                      4 × sin(4 × 180 ÷ π)

which is equivalent to the calculation of F.
